drifted.spec_text: 1 to 32 fixed extent size classes, configurable at initialization, up to 10M slots per class
drifted.actual: Dynamic slab-based allocation: initialize(total_size_bytes, slab_size_bytes, ns_id). Slabs allocated on-demand, max 256 slabs. No pre-declared size classes or slot counts.

drifted.spec_text: System MUST use a logger receptacle for all console/diagnostic output
drifted.actual: Logger receptacle declared but never read or used. Type mismatch between interfaces::ILogger and example_logger::ILogger prevents external binding.

drifted.spec_text: At least 32 distinct size classes with up to 10M slots per class
drifted.actual: Max 256 slabs (dynamic), ~262K slots per 1 GiB slab. Different capacity model.

drifted.spec_text: Wire full component stack: Logger, SPDKEnv, BlockDeviceSpdkNvme, ExtentManagerComponentV1
drifted.actual: Logger not bound to ExtentManagerComponentV1 due to ILogger type mismatch. Bound to spdk_env and block_dev only.

drifted.spec_text: N threads complete approximately N times faster for lookup operations
drifted.actual: Lookup latency rounds to 0us at microsecond resolution, making linear scaling claim untestable via this tool.

conflicts.description: 001 FR-007 vs implementation: Spec describes static size classes at init; code uses dynamic slab allocation with different initialize() signature.
001 FR-014 vs 002 FR-009: Logger receptacle type mismatch (interfaces::ILogger vs example_logger::ILogger) makes binding impossible.
